name: tdd-pytest
description: Python/pytest TDD specialist for test-driven development workflows. Use
capabilities: Guide Red-Green-Refactor TDD workflow for Python projects
Generate pytest test files with fixtures and parametrization
Configure pytest settings in pyproject.toml files
Audit existing test suites for quality and coverage gaps
Create test reports in TESTING_REPORT.local.md format
Provide pytest command examples for coverage analysis
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation in SKILL.md with pytest examples and TDD methodology instructions. All static findings are false positives from code examples in markdown backticks. No executable code, network calls, or filesystem operations exist in the skill.
valueStatement: Writing tests first helps catch bugs early and improves code design. This skill guides you through test-driven development cycles with pytest, from writing failing tests to achieving green coverage.
userTitle: Write Python Tests Using TDD and Pytest
limitations: Does not execute tests directly, relies on Claude Code tools
Requires pytest and dependencies already installed in environment
Cannot modify production code automatically without user approval
Focuses on pytest framework, not unittest or other test tools

useCases.title: Add tests for new features
useCases.description: Follow Red-Green-Refactor cycle when building new functionality to ensure code correctness from the start.

useCases.title: Audit test suite quality
useCases.description: Review existing tests for coverage gaps, missing edge cases, and adherence to pytest best practices.

useCases.title: Configure CI test pipelines
useCases.description: Set up pytest configuration and generate structured test reports for continuous integration workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Write first failing test
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a failing test for a function that validates email addresses. Show me the RED phase first.

promptTemplates.title: Run tests with coverage
promptTemplates.prompt: Run pytest with coverage on my tests directory and show which lines need test coverage.

promptTemplates.title: Create test fixtures
promptTemplates.prompt: Create pytest fixtures for mocking a database connection, including setup and teardown logic.

promptTemplates.title: Review pytest config
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my pyproject.toml pytest settings and suggest improvements for async tests and coverage reporting.

bestPractices: Always write the failing test first before implementing functionality (RED phase)
Keep each test focused on a single behavior or assertion to maintain clarity
Use pytest fixtures with appropriate scopes to share setup logic across tests
antiPatterns: Writing implementation code before seeing the test fail for the right reason
Creating tests that depend on execution order or shared mutable state
Skipping edge cases and error conditions when writing test scenarios

faq.question: Does this skill install pytest automatically?
faq.answer: No. You must have pytest installed. Use uv add pytest or pip install pytest before using this skill.

faq.question: Can I use this with Django or FastAPI projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides pytest guidance applicable to any Python framework. Configure framework-specific plugins in pyproject.toml.

faq.question: How does this differ from writing tests without TDD?
faq.answer: TDD requires writing failing tests first, which improves design and ensures tests actually validate behavior instead of passing incorrectly.

faq.question: Will this skill modify my source code?
faq.answer: The skill guides test writing but does not automatically modify code. All changes require your review through Claude Code interface.

faq.question: Can I run tests in parallel with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides guidance on using pytest-xdist plugin with -n auto flag for parallel test execution.

faq.question: What if my tests keep failing?
faq.answer: Check pytest output for the specific assertion failure. Common issues: wrong expected values, missing imports, or incorrect test setup.
